4十-—•

Arbosa s

￥.~

Allar p.

JUN 1- 174€

i Charit

....나라

DEPARTMENT OF STATE

WASHINGTON

dune # , 13:*

My dear Mr. Ambassador:

I wish to refer to your letter dated May 27, 1946 regarding the transfer into dollare of francs which American film companies will earn in their operations under the agreement between our two

Governmente on the showing of American films In France signed May 28, 1946.

Your letter states that the sum of $3 million of earnings per year which you propose as a possible maximum for transter in the period July 1, 1946 to July 1, 1948 is approximately equal to the amount that would

be

His Excellency

Henri Bonnet,

Ambassador of the French Republic.

•

-2-

be transferred 1f no maximum were imposed, 1.e., under the continued application of the terme of the letter of assurances addressed by M. Pleven to Mr. Vinson on

September 26, 1945 which provides for transfer of total current payments, including profits.

In view of these circumstances, I suggest that consideration of establishing a maximum sum on such transfers be postponed for a reasonable period until the amount of earnings of the American film companies can be more accurately determined.

similar_documents.title: Lettre d'Henri Bonnet à William Clayton relative au transfert des revenus nets courants revenant à des compagnies cinématographiques ayant leur siège aux États-Unis au titre des bénéfices réalisés par elles en France
